title: Spermacoce neohispida
taxonomy: Spermacoce neohispida is classified within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It belongs to the genus Spermacoce, which consists of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Spermacoce is part of the tribe Spermacoceae, which is a group of flowering plants within the family Rubiaceae. The tribe Spermacoceae is further classified within the subfamily Rubioideae, which is one of the largest subfamilies within the Rubiaceae family. Further classification includes the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants.
uses: Spermacoce neohispida is reported to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. The plant is said to have antipyretic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. It is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, infections, and digestive problems. The plant's leaves and roots are used to prepare remedies, often in combination with other herbs. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Spermacoce neohispida for medicinal purposes.
